title: A Toll on Every Hull

mood.label: Heavy going 🌧️
mood.summary: The world holds level at 38 as the US-Iran war grinds into a third night of American strikes and hardens into a reinstated Hormuz blockade on July 14 — a 20-percent toll demanded on every passing hull, Brent above 83, the S&P off 0.79 percent and the Nikkei down over four — with only a thin thread of promised Switzerland talks running against the escalation, more deadly Russia-Ukraine salvos, a Toronto festival shooting, a 700-death Congo Ebola outbreak and a thousand-year Missouri flood on the far side of the ledger.

style.genre: harp-led dark chamber post-rock / drone — a sampled gm_orchestral_harp plucking a circling Ab phrygian topline over a slow bowed-string drone, a plucked gm_acoustic_bass walking the low roots with a sine sub beneath, a muffled RolandTR909 heartbeat kick and a distant AkaiLinn cross-stick through deep tape-echo, and sparse glockenspiel glints for the day's few bright threads; phrygian's flat second (A natural leaning against the Ab root) tilting the whole thing toward dread without collapse — a blockade-morning day held as weight rather than dramatized, and deliberately no piano or clarinet after yesterday's dirge

sampleSources: Strudel default library (gm_orchestral_harp lead, gm_acoustic_bass, VCSL strings & glockenspiel, RolandTR909 & AkaiLinn drum machines, sine sub)
pattern:
stack(
  s("bd ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ bd ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~").bank("RolandTR909").lpf(160).gain(0.5).room(0.35).rsize(4),
  s("~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ rim ~ ~ ~").bank("AkaiLinn").gain(0.24).room(0.45).pan(0.58).delay(0.28).delaytime(0.5),
  note("<[af2,b3,ef4] [a2,df4,e4] [af2,b3,ef4] [gf2,df4,gf4]>").s("strings").attack(1.6).sustain(0.85).release(3.2).gain(0.15).lpf(sine.range(400,1100).slow(24)).room(0.6).rsize(5).hpf(110),
  note("<af2 a2 af2 gf2>").s("gm_acoustic_bass").attack(0.01).release(1.4).gain(0.3).lpf(900).room(0.3).off(0.5, x=>x.gain(0.09).lpf(700)),
  note("<[af4 ~ ~ e4 ~ df4 ~ b3 ~ ~ a3 ~ af3 ~ ~ ~] [ef4 ~ ~ df4 ~ b3 ~ a3 ~ ~ b3 ~ df4 ~ ~ ~]>").s("gm_orchestral_harp").attack(0.005).release(0.9).gain(perlin.range(0.28,0.42).slow(9)).lpf(2600).room(0.5).delay(0.24).delaytime(0.28).off(0.125, x=>x.gain(0.1).s("glockenspiel").lpf(3400)),
  note("~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ af5 ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ef5 ~ ~ ~").s("glockenspiel").gain(0.16).room(0.55).delay(0.3).delaytime(0.5).pan(0.64),
  note("<af1 a1 af1 gf1>").s("sine").gain(0.32).lpf(150).attack(0.06).release(1.9)
)

lines: Third night the fire came back down over the coast and gate,
and the blockade shut the strait again on the morning of the fourteenth.
Every loaded hull that crosses leaves a fifth of what it's worth —
thirty million on a supertanker for a mile of open water.
It's thirty-eight and heavy, the gate is closed once more,
Brent climbs over eighty-three and the chip stocks hit the floor.
Kuwait and Bahrain, Oman, Qatar caught the answering fire,
but somebody's booking Switzerland for a table by the week's end wire.
Moscow, Belgorod, Zaporizhzhia — nine names gone in the night,
and a salsa street in Toronto lost two before the lights.
Congo's counting seven hundred graves, Gaza past seventy-three,
and Black Hawks lifted the camp kids clear of a thousand-year Missouri.
It's thirty-eight and holding low, the heavy side runs wide,
but France will meet Spain in Dallas, the last four top-ranked, side by side.
A thin thread found the Sun's own seam, an Ebola trial began —
small stubborn lights that couldn't lift a blockade-morning span.
